compact_for_parent: Pass 1 (security-governance) | HIGH | 3 must_fix, 3 should_fix | Key: SQL/Storage public access not disabled; GDPR processor validation missing; PCI scope unproven

findings.title: Private endpoints are specified without explicitly shutting off SQL and Storage public access
findings.recommendation: Require explicit public-access shutdown for Azure SQL and Storage, document private DNS and pe-subnet controls.

findings.title: GDPR residency and processor obligations are asserted, not validated
findings.recommendation: Add processor-by-processor residency and legal-basis matrix, validate external tenant geography, document SCC/DPA requirements.

findings.title: The PCI token-only model is not proven by the proposed payment flow
findings.recommendation: Mandate hosted payment fields or redirect tokenization, block payment payload logging, document PCI scope and SAQ boundary.

findings.title: Azure Policy compliance is presented as covered before live discovery occurs
findings.recommendation: Add governance-discovery gate before implementation readiness. Mark policy compliance as provisional.

findings.title: Key Vault is hardened for RBAC and recovery, but not for network isolation
findings.recommendation: Add Key Vault private endpoint or firewall; state soft-delete retention and purge/recovery role model.

findings.title: Managed Identity and privileged identity scoping are too vague
findings.recommendation: Define least-privilege roles for MI, separate customer vs workforce identity, keep admin in workforce tenant with MFA/PIM.
